items.question: Who can register on SEARN?
items.answer: SEARN is open to regulators, NRA staff, training providers, and authorized WHO partners across the South-East Asia region.

items.question: Is the platform free to use?
items.answer: Yes, SEARN is provided free of charge to eligible regulators and NRA staff as part of WHO's regional capacity-building mandate.

items.question: How do I sign up?
items.answer: Click 'Sign up' from the login page, complete your details, and verify your mobile via OTP. Your NRA administrator will approve your role assignment.

items.question: How are trainings assigned?
items.answer: Trainings can be self-enrolled from the catalogue, or assigned by your NRA Manager based on identified capability gaps.

items.question: How do I get my certificate?
items.answer: Once a training is marked Completed and your proof of completion is uploaded and verified, your certificate is automatically added to your Regulatory Passport.

items.question: Can I retake a training?
items.answer: Yes, most trainings can be revisited at any time. Your latest completion status is recorded against your competency record.

items.question: What is a capability gap?
items.answer: A capability gap is a defined area where a regulator's current competency is below the proficiency level required for their role. SEARN maps each gap to specific learning activities.

items.question: Who assigns capability gaps?
items.answer: Your NRA Manager assigns capability gaps and tracks your progress as you complete the linked competencies and activities.

items.question: I forgot my password — what do I do?
items.answer: Click 'Forgot password?' on the login page and follow the email recovery link to set a new password.

items.question: Is my data secure?
items.answer: Yes. We use industry-standard encryption, role-based access controls, and follow WHO data-protection guidelines. See our Privacy Policy for details.

items.question: Can I sign in with my organization SSO?
items.answer: Yes — use the 'Login with WHO' button on the login page if your organization has SSO enabled.
